segments.caption: The video opens with a sweeping aerial view of a dense urban skyline, likely New York City, showcasing towering skyscrapers, waterfront greenery, and boats along the river under a bright blue sky with scattered clouds. The scene transitions to a high-angle shot of a narrow city street flanked by ornate buildings, with cars moving along the road. Next, the perspective shifts to the interior of a bustling multi-level shopping mall, filled with escalators, shops, and people. This is followed by a rapid transition to a grassy, overgrown field with a dramatic sunset, then to a busy underground transit station with blurred motion of commuters walking past. The video then cuts to a flooded street with submerged cars, indicating severe weather or flooding. The scene changes to a man in a dark blue shirt and glasses speaking directly to the camera in an office setting, with a bookshelf and a map of the United States behind him. The final frames show a close-up of water flowing through a concrete channel, followed by the logo for 'Practical Engineering' with an orange hard hat and glasses icon on a light wood background.

segments.caption: The video opens with the Practical Engineering logo displayed on a light wood background, featuring an orange hard hat with glasses and the text 'PRACTICAL ENGINEERING', followed by a sponsorship acknowledgment to CuriosityStream. The scene transitions to a close-up of water flowing through a concrete channel, suggesting a drainage system. Next, the Speaker in Office appears, standing in front of a bookshelf and a map of the United States, speaking directly to the camera. The video then cuts to a rainy outdoor scene viewed through a window, showing trees and a wet landscape, followed by a shot of a person riding a bicycle through a rain-soaked street. This is followed by water gushing from a downspout attached to a house, then a large concrete pipe discharging water into a stream. The perspective shifts to a rainy cityscape viewed through a rain-speckled window, showing buildings and a highway. A close-up of rainwater pooling on a sidewalk next to grass is shown, followed by a dramatic shot of a flooded street with turbulent water. The final scene depicts cars driving through a flooded urban street at night, their headlights reflecting off the water, emphasizing the severity of the flooding.

segments.caption: The video opens with a nighttime scene of a flooded urban street, where cars navigate through deep water under the glow of headlights and traffic lights, capturing the chaos of a storm. The scene transitions to a split-screen view: on the left, heavy rain falls on lush green trees, while on the right, a car drives through a muddy, flooded roadside ditch, emphasizing the severity of the weather. Next, a close-up shows water forcefully flowing from a concrete drainage pipe into a channel, highlighting infrastructure under stress. The video then cuts to a man in an office setting—identified as the Speaker in Office—wearing glasses and a dark blue button-up shirt, speaking directly to the camera in front of a bookshelf and a map of the United States. He appears to be explaining or narrating, possibly about the flooding or drainage systems. The scene shifts again to a downspout discharging rainwater onto a stone-paved area, illustrating residential runoff. Finally, the video shows a detailed architectural blueprint labeled 'MAIN FLOOR PLAN' with annotations for a 'TRENCH DRAIN SYSTEM,' suggesting a technical or engineering perspective on managing water flow in construction design.

segments.caption: The video begins with a close-up of an architectural blueprint labeled 'MAIN FLOOR PLAN' with annotations for a 'TRENCH DRAIN SYSTEM,' suggesting engineering design for stormwater management. This transitions to the cover of the 'New York City STORMWATER DESIGN MANUAL,' featuring the Statue of Liberty and dolphins in the water, emphasizing urban water management. The narrative then shifts to historical black-and-white footage of a flooded city street, where people navigate in a rowboat past a 'FURNITURE STORE' sign, illustrating past flood impacts. This is followed by a modern aerial view of a residential neighborhood submerged in floodwaters, highlighting the severity of contemporary flooding. The scene then focuses on a clogged storm drain with debris, showing water overflowing and flowing rapidly, underscoring infrastructure strain. A diagram illustrates water flow between two houses, with arrows indicating direction, followed by a real-world shot of a wet road with cars driving through puddles, reinforcing the theme of surface runoff. The segment concludes with a close-up of rainwater cascading over a curb, emphasizing the continuous challenge of stormwater management in urban environments.

segments.caption: The video begins with a close-up of turbulent, foamy water flowing rapidly, likely from a storm drain or overflow, setting a tone of urban water management challenges. The scene transitions to the Speaker in Office, a bald man in glasses and a dark blue button-up shirt, standing in front of a bookshelf and a map of the United States, speaking directly to the camera—likely explaining water infrastructure concepts. The video then cuts to an aerial view of a large circular water treatment basin with greenish water and a central rotating mechanism, indicating a wastewater treatment facility. This is followed by a ground-level shot of raw, murky water gushing forcefully from large pipes into a concrete channel, emphasizing the volume and force of stormwater or sewage discharge. The next scene shows a wet city street with rainwater pooling and flowing toward a storm drain, overlaid with the text 'Municipal Separate Storm Sewer System (MS4)', clarifying the system being discussed. The video continues with a stone culvert discharging water into a small stream, then a close-up of water flowing from a concrete pipe into a rocky area, and finally, a rapid current in a natural stream, illustrating the journey of stormwater from urban systems to natural waterways.

segments.caption: The video begins with a close-up of a clogged storm drain on a wet concrete surface, where water flows rapidly around and over a metal grate partially blocked by brown leaves, indicating surface runoff during rainfall. The scene transitions to a murky stream where a crumpled green and white plastic container floats, suggesting pollution in the water. Next, a large concrete pipe discharges water into a stream, creating a small waterfall over rocks, highlighting stormwater outflow. The video then cuts to a man in an office setting—identified as the Speaker in Office—wearing glasses and a dark blue button-up shirt, speaking directly to the camera in front of a bookshelf and a map of the United States. He appears to be explaining a topic, likely related to stormwater management, given the preceding visuals. The final scene shows a wide, concrete-lined water channel under a clear blue sky, with calm water reflecting the surroundings and palm trees visible along the banks, suggesting a managed urban drainage system in a dry climate, possibly a river or flood control channel.

segments.caption: The video begins with a wide shot of a concrete-lined Water Channel under a clear blue sky, flanked by palm trees and buildings, suggesting an urban drainage system. The scene transitions to a close-up of a transparent tank where a hand places a circuit board vertically into flowing water, simulating a test environment for water flow or erosion. The board remains upright as water flows past it, indicating a controlled experiment. The video then cuts to an aerial view of a large urban Water Channel running parallel to train tracks and highways, emphasizing its integration into city infrastructure. This is followed by a natural river scene with muddy water rushing around tree trunks, highlighting natural water flow in contrast to engineered systems. The next shot shows a narrow, concrete-lined channel with stagnant, algae-covered water, possibly indicating neglect or low flow. The video then shifts to an aerial view of a large excavation site with a water-filled pit, likely a stormwater retention basin under construction, with heavy machinery visible. This is followed by a Google Earth satellite image of a similar retention basin in a commercial area, surrounded by parking lots and roads, illustrating real-world stormwater management. The segment concludes with a simple animated diagram showing water inflow and outflow in a channel, labeled 'INFLOW' and 'OUTFLOW', providing a schematic explanation of the system’s function.

segments.caption: The video begins with an animated diagram illustrating water flow dynamics in a channel, labeled 'INFLOW' and 'OUTFLOW', with a graph above showing two curves (blue and orange) representing inflow and outflow rates over time. The water level in the channel rises and falls in sync with the graph, visually demonstrating how inflow peaks before outflow, indicating a retention or delay effect. The scene then transitions to a man in an office setting, identified as the 'Speaker in Office', who is bald, wears glasses, and a dark blue button-up shirt, speaking directly to the camera in front of a bookshelf and a map of the United States. Following this, the video cuts to a series of natural and urban scenes: a close-up of fish swimming in shallow, clear water; a lush forest waterfall surrounded by greenery; a fast-flowing river through a dense forest; an aerial view of a sunlit suburban area with trees and houses; a close-up of colorful flowers (marigolds, petunias) near a puddle; and finally, water being poured from a copper-colored spout into a blue bucket, with a green hose visible, suggesting rainwater collection or irrigation. The sequence moves from technical explanation to real-world applications and natural water systems, emphasizing water management and environmental context.

segments.caption: The video opens with a wide shot of a green rooftop garden in an urban setting, featuring dense clusters of small white flowers and a backdrop of tall skyscrapers, including a building labeled 'Water Tower Place'. The scene transitions to a calm body of water, likely a lake or river, bordered by trees and a grassy park area with benches and people walking in the distance. Next, a close-up shows water being poured from a green hose onto a circular, porous stone-like structure placed in a metal tray, demonstrating water absorption or filtration. The camera zooms in further to show water flowing from a metal spout into a bed of small, rounded stones, emphasizing infiltration. The scene then cuts to a man in a dark blue button-up shirt and glasses, standing in an office with a bookshelf and a framed map of the United States behind him, speaking directly to the camera—this is the 'Speaker in Office'. The video then shifts to a flooded road with orange 'CAUTION' barricades, followed by a car submerged up to its roof in murky water, with a person in a high-visibility vest attempting to assist. The final scenes show a white car driving through deep floodwaters at night, creating large splashes, and a first-person view from inside a vehicle navigating through rushing water, highlighting the dangers of driving during severe flooding.

segments.caption: The video opens with a dramatic, first-person perspective from inside a vehicle navigating a heavily flooded street, with turbulent water splashing against the side mirror and windows, emphasizing the danger of driving through floodwaters. This transitions to a daytime scene of a white car partially submerged in floodwater, with a person in a bright yellow safety jacket wading through the water to assist, highlighting emergency response efforts. The next scene shows a rural road blocked by floodwaters, with orange and black barricades labeled 'CAUTION' placed at the edge, indicating a closed route due to flooding. The video then cuts to a close-up of a car driving through a flooded residential street, with water surging around the vehicle, reinforcing the severity of the flood. The scene shifts dramatically to an aerial view of Central Park in New York City, showcasing its green fields, lakes, and surrounding urban skyline under clear skies, providing a stark contrast to the earlier flood scenes. This is followed by a nighttime cityscape with illuminated buildings and streets under a cloudy sky, suggesting urban life continuing despite weather challenges. The final segment features a bald man in glasses and a dark blue button-up shirt, identified as the 'Speaker in Office', speaking directly to the camera in a well-lit office with a bookshelf and a framed map of the United States in the background, likely discussing stormwater management or flood-related topics.

segments.caption: The video begins with a close-up of a person wearing a yellow apron and gloves, using a trowel to mix a granular, grayish material in a black tray, likely a construction or landscaping substance. The scene quickly transitions to a promotional segment for Nebula, a video platform, showcasing its interface with various content thumbnails, including 'Thomas Frank visits Gravity Falls' and '16 Days in Berlin'. The promotion highlights benefits like 'No pre-roll ads', 'Support creators', and 'Download videos', and then introduces a partnership with CuriosityStream, displaying their combined offerings of documentaries and indie content. The segment ends with a dynamic title sequence featuring the text 'ANCIENT EARTH' over a dramatic, rocky landscape, followed by a serene aerial shot of a lush, green park with a winding path and a small body of water, suggesting a shift to a nature or environmental theme.

segments.caption: The video begins with a breathtaking aerial view of a futuristic cityscape under a starry sky, featuring large, dome-like structures and lush greenery, transitioning into a cosmic scene with Earth from space and the CuriosityStream logo emerging. The scene shifts to a digital interface showcasing the CuriosityStream platform, highlighting various documentary thumbnails such as 'Hubble's Imager' and 'Masters Of Time: Independent Watchmakers,' with a call to action to sign up. The interface then displays a series of video thumbnails from 'Tom Scott Presents: Money,' followed by a promotional screen for '16 Days in Berlin' on a sleek monitor. The video concludes with the Nebula and CuriosityStream logos side by side, followed by the Practical Engineering logo, a list of Patreon supporters, and credits for the video's production team, including Grady Hillhouse and Wesley Crump, with a copyright notice for 2020.

entity_library.Practical Engineering Logo: A logo featuring an orange hard hat with glasses, the text 'PRACTICAL ENGINEERING,' and a dotted line with arrows on a light wood background, with additional text thanking CuriosityStream for sponsoring the video.
entity_library.Rainy Outdoor Scene: A view through a window showing trees and a wet landscape during heavy rain, with raindrops visible on the glass.
entity_library.Bicyclist in Rain: A person riding a bicycle through a rain-soaked street, with only the lower body and bike visible, emphasizing the wet conditions.
entity_library.Downspout Discharge: Water gushing from a downspout attached to a house, with rainwater flowing heavily into a garden area.
entity_library.Concrete Pipe Outflow: A large concrete pipe discharging water into a stream, creating a small waterfall over rocks, with surrounding greenery.
entity_library.Rainwater on Sidewalk: A close-up of rainwater pooling on a concrete sidewalk next to a patch of green grass, highlighting surface runoff.
entity_library.Nighttime Flooded Street: An urban street at night submerged in water, with cars driving through, their headlights reflecting off the surface, and traffic lights visible.
entity_library.Architectural Blueprint: A detailed technical drawing labeled 'MAIN FLOOR PLAN' with annotations for a 'TRENCH DRAIN SYSTEM', indicating engineering design for water management.
entity_library.New York City STORMWATER DESIGN MANUAL: A document cover featuring the Statue of Liberty and dolphins in the water, with the title 'New York City STORMWATER DESIGN MANUAL' and the NYC Environmental Protection logo.
entity_library.Historical Flood Scene: Black-and-white footage of a flooded city street with people in a rowboat, a 'FURNITURE STORE' sign, and debris in the water, depicting past flood events.
entity_library.Aerial Flooded Neighborhood: An overhead view of a residential area with houses and yards submerged in floodwaters, showing modern flood impacts.
entity_library.Clogged Storm Drain: A close-up of a storm drain with a metal grate partially blocked by brown leaves, with water flowing rapidly around and over it on a wet concrete surface.
entity_library.Water Flow Diagram: A schematic illustration showing water flow between two houses, with blue arrows indicating direction and a dotted line representing water movement.
entity_library.Municipal Separate Storm Sewer System (M: Text overlay on a wet street scene, identifying the type of stormwater management system being shown, with rainwater flowing toward a storm drain.
entity_library.Circuit Board Test Setup: A hand placing a circuit board vertically into a transparent tank with flowing water, simulating a controlled experiment for water flow or erosion testing.
entity_library.Excavation Site with Retention Basin: An aerial view of a large excavation site with a water-filled pit, likely a stormwater retention basin under construction, with heavy machinery visible.
entity_library.Google Earth Satellite View: A satellite image from Google Earth showing a stormwater retention basin in a commercial area, surrounded by parking lots and roads, illustrating real-world stormwater management.
entity_library.Animated Water Flow Diagram: A simple animated diagram showing water inflow and outflow in a channel, labeled 'INFLOW' and 'OUTFLOW', with a graph above depicting the timing and volume of water flow, illustrating a retention or delay effect in stormwater systems.
entity_library.Fish in Shallow Water: A close-up of fish swimming in clear, shallow water over a rocky, algae-covered bed, with sunlight reflecting off the surface, indicating a natural aquatic environment.
entity_library.Forest Waterfall: A tall, narrow waterfall cascading down a rock face in a lush green forest, surrounded by trees and moss-covered rocks, with a small stream flowing out at the base.
entity_library.Fast-Flowing River in Forest: A turbulent, white-water river rushing through a dense forest, flanked by rocks, fallen logs, and green foliage, showcasing natural water movement in a mountainous or hilly terrain.
entity_library.Aerial View of Suburban Area: An aerial shot of a residential neighborhood during golden hour, with trees, houses, and streets bathed in warm sunlight, suggesting a peaceful, green urban environment, though this appears to be a misidentification as the actual scene shown is Central Park in New York City.
entity_library.Colorful Flowers by Puddle: A vibrant cluster of orange marigolds, purple and pink petunias, and white flowers growing near a shallow puddle, with raindrops visible on the water surface, indicating recent rainfall or irrigation.
entity_library.Water Being Poured into Bucket: Water flowing from a copper-colored spout into a blue plastic bucket, with a green hose nearby, suggesting rainwater harvesting or manual watering, possibly for gardening or irrigation.
entity_library.Rooftop Garden: A green rooftop covered in dense clusters of small white flowers, bordered by a city skyline, serving as a natural stormwater management feature.
entity_library.Porous Stone Structure: A circular, textured stone-like object placed in a metal tray, designed to absorb or filter water, shown being irrigated with a hose.
entity_library.Metal Spout Drainage: A close-up of a metal spout discharging water into a bed of small, rounded stones, illustrating a controlled water infiltration system.
entity_library.Central Park Aerial View: An aerial shot of Central Park in New York City, showing its expansive green fields, lakes, and surrounding dense urban skyline under clear skies, providing a serene contrast to the earlier flood scenes.
entity_library.Nebula: A video platform interface with a clean, white background, featuring a 'Why Subscribe?' section with icons for 'No pre-roll ads,' 'Support creators,' and 'Download videos,' and thumbnails such as 'Thomas Frank visits Gravity Falls' and '16 Days in Berlin.'
entity_library.CuriosityStream: A video platform interface with a dark theme, displaying documentary thumbnails like 'Hubble's Imager' and 'Masters Of Time: Independent Watchmakers,' along with 'Sign In' and 'Sign Up' buttons, and a preview notice.
entity_library.Nebula Interface: A clean, modern video platform interface with a white background, featuring a 'Why Subscribe?' section with icons for 'No pre-roll ads,' 'Support creators,' and 'Download videos,' and a grid of video thumbnails including 'Thomas Frank visits Gravity Falls' and '16 Days in Berlin.'
entity_library.CuriosityStream Interface: A dark-themed video platform interface with a grid of documentary thumbnails such as 'Deep Time History,' 'Hubble's Imager,' and 'Innovation Nation,' displayed on multiple devices including a TV, laptop, tablet, and smartphone.
entity_library.ANCIENT EARTH Title Sequence: A dramatic title card with the text 'ANCIENT EARTH' in bold, black letters over a rocky, barren landscape under a cloudy sky, transitioning into a serene aerial view of a green park with a winding path and water feature.
entity_library.Tom Scott Presents: Money: A video series on the Nebula platform, featuring thumbnails for episodes titled 'The Draw,' 'The Bid,' 'The Magic Money Machine,' and 'The Negotiation,' with a description about trust, negotiation, and money.
entity_library.16 Days in Berlin: A Nebula Original documentary with a dramatic cover image of a war-torn cityscape, tanks, and soldiers, subtitled 'Final Battle of WW2 Day by Day,' displayed prominently on a monitor interface.
entity_library.Patreon Supporters List: A white screen listing numerous names of supporters who contributed to the video's production via Patreon and YouTube, arranged in multiple columns.
